children.text: to conserve, protect, and enhance for the benefit and enjoyment of present and future generations the ecological, scenic, wildlife, recreational, cultural, historical, natural, educational, and scientific resources of the National Conservation Area; and

children.label: A
children.text: located in the National Conservation Area; and

children.label: B
children.text: listed as a threatened or endangered species on the list of threatened species or the list of endangered species published under .section 1533(c)(1) of this title

children.heading: Habitat conservation plan
children.label: 1 Habitat conservation plan
children.text: The term “habitat conservation plan” means the conservation plan entitled “Washington County Habitat Conservation Plan” and dated .February 23, 1996

children.heading: Management plan
children.label: 2 Management plan
children.text: The term “management plan” means the management plan for the National Conservation Area developed by the Secretary under subsection (d)(1).

children.label: A
children.text: consists of approximately 44,725 acres of public land in the County, as generally depicted on the Red Cliffs National Conservation Area Map; and

children.label: B
children.text: is established by subsection (c).

children.heading: Public use plan
children.label: 4 Public use plan
children.text: The term “public use plan” means the use plan entitled “Red Cliffs Desert Reserve Public Use Plan” and dated , as amended.June 12, 2000

children.heading: Resource management plan
children.label: 5 Resource management plan
children.text: The term “resource management plan” means the management plan entitled “St. George Field Office Resource Management Plan” and dated , as amended.March 15, 1999

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: c Establishment
children.text: Subject to valid existing rights, there is established in the State the Red Cliffs National Conservation Area.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than 3 years after , and in accordance with paragraph (2), the Secretary shall develop a comprehensive plan for the long-term management of the National Conservation Area.March 30, 2009

children.heading: Uses
children.label: 2 Uses
children.text: The Secretary shall only allow uses of the National Conservation Area that the Secretary determines would further a purpose described in subsection (a).

children.heading: Motorized vehicles
children.label: 3 Motorized vehicles
children.text: Except in cases in which motorized vehicles are needed for administrative purposes, or to respond to an emergency, the use of motorized vehicles in the National Conservation Area shall be permitted only on roads designated by the management plan for the use of motorized vehicles.

children.label: i
children.text: such reasonable regulations, policies, and practices as the Secretary considers necessary; and

children.label: ii
children.text: applicable law; and

children.label: B
children.text: in a manner consistent with the purposes described in subsection (a).

children.heading: Wildland fire operations
children.label: 5 Wildland fire operations
children.text: Nothing in this section prohibits the Secretary, in cooperation with other Federal, State, and local agencies, as appropriate, from conducting wildland fire operations in the National Conservation Area, consistent with the purposes of this section.

children.heading: Incorporation of acquired land and interests
children.label: f Incorporation of acquired land and interests

children.text: become part of the National Conservation Area; and

children.label: A
children.text: all forms of entry, appropriation, and disposal under the public land laws;

children.label: B

children.label: C

children.heading: Additional land
children.label: 2 Additional land
children.text: If the Secretary acquires additional land that is located in the National Conservation Area after , the land is withdrawn from operation of the laws referred to in paragraph (1) on the date of acquisition of the land.March 30, 2009
